title: [Bug]: Named stages are not resolved if they are referenced using a variable
body:
### Issue Description

Referencing build stages using a variable (e.g. `FROM base-${VERSION}`) does not work. Podman tries to find an image matching the provided name instead of checking for build stages first.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Build the following containerfile
```Containerfile
ARG ALPINE=false

FROM alpine:latest as alpine-true

FROM scratch as alpine-false

# this does not work
FROM alpine-${ALPINE}
# this works
# FROM alpine-false

COPY / /
```

### Describe the results you received

```
[3/3] STEP 1/2: FROM alpine-false
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine-false:latest...
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
[2/3] STEP 1/1: FROM scratch AS alpine-false
--> a842c75bc41
[3/3] STEP 1/2: FROM a842c75bc414479a092033cf42ae34320dce4aaf6e79969779b6a3780310b470
[3/3] STEP 2/2: COPY / /
[3/3] COMMIT
--> b30e71188d6
b30e71188d6fb2e3a65643de8028412e6fca049c723c7c24f18063228998daa0
```

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I encountered this issue trying to build [spilo](https://github.com/zalando/spilo).

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc could you see if this has been fixed?

I can no longer reproduce with podman 4.4.1, so I think this issue can be closed.

Any idea how this got fixed?
Must be fixed either in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4366 or https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4401
